description_long: This is a fragment of a ceramic bowl, characterized by a thick, rounded rim and a shallow, concave interior. The exterior surface is a natural, earthy brown, while the interior is coated with a pale, off-white glaze that shows some pitting and discoloration. The rim is uneven and jagged, indicating that the bowl is broken and only a portion remains. There are two small, circular marks on the interior surface, possibly from manufacturing or use. The fragment is relatively thick, suggesting it was a sturdy vessel. A handwritten identification number is visible on the inner surface near the rim.
description: A broken ceramic bowl fragment with a thick, rounded rim and a pale glazed interior, showing signs of wear and pitting.
description_view_0: Front view showing the concave interior, thick rim, and two small marks on the glazed surface.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the curvature of the rim and the thickness of the ceramic fragment.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the exterior curve and the overall crescent shape of the broken bowl.
description_view_3: Opposite angled view showing the interior glaze and the jagged, broken edge of the rim.
